title: Sequestered live-test stage
source: maxdama.pdf §5.5 Michael Dubno interview + r/quant top thread (top comment by u/WhoIsTheUnPerson) — both independently identify this as the gap between backtest and production.
why: The pipeline ends at WF backtest. Dubno: *"Backtesting, paper trading, and live trading are three worlds. The system should not be able to tell which world it is in."* The current code architecture can support paper trading with minor extension; doing it before Paso 2 multi-asset is probably out of scope, but the design should be sketched now so the orchestrator doesn't lock in shape that breaks live-mode.
value_rationale: M (downgraded from H in 2026-05-27 recalibration — still Paso 2/3 prereq for any live work, but no audit-passed primary exists yet to paper-trade; gated behind B0040+B0032+at-least-one-Loop-A-survivor-clearing-full-audit)
scope: new `scripts/paper_trade.py` reusing `_run_one_primary` but ingesting live MT5 ticks; same OHLCV contract; signals go to a paper-broker stub instead of historical backtest.
